title: JIT IR translation — map operations
purpose: Gleam uses maps extensively for record-like data structures. Map construction (put_map_assoc, put_map_exact), update, and pattern matching (get_map_elements, has_map_fields) are common in Gleam code. JIT-compiling these to native code eliminates interpreter dispatch overhead. Map operations are complex (hash tables, key comparison, structural sharing) and compile to runtime helper calls rather than inline IR — the performance gain is from avoiding the interpreter's opcode dispatch loop, not from optimising the map operations themselves.
task: Implement IR translation for map opcodes in crates/beamr/src/jit/ir_map.rs. All map operations compile to runtime helper calls. Construction and update operations allocate on the heap (require GC safepoints). Pattern matching operations are read-only (no safepoints). This brief makes maps no longer require deoptimisation to the interpreter.

requirements.title: Map construction IR — put_map_assoc
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate put_map_assoc into Cranelift IR that calls jit_map_new(process, pairs_ptr, pair_count) runtime helper. THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a GC safepoint before the call (heap allocation). THE SYSTEM SHALL pass key-value pairs as an array of u64 term values. THE SYSTEM SHALL track all key and value Terms as GC roots across the allocation.
acceptance: translate_put_map_assoc(builder, emitter, pairs, dest, fail_label) emits safepoint + jit_map_new call
jit_map_new(process, pairs_ptr, pair_count) allocates a new map on the process heap
Keys and values are in safepoint root set
Result is a tagged boxed map Term
Test: compiled #{a => 1, b => 2} -> valid 2-entry map
Test: compiled empty map #{} -> valid empty map

requirements.title: Map update IR — put_map_exact
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate put_map_exact into Cranelift IR that calls jit_map_update(process, existing_map, pairs_ptr, pair_count) runtime helper. THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a GC safepoint before the call. IF a key does not exist in the map (exact update semantics), THE SYSTEM SHALL branch to the fail label (badmap error). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT modify the existing map — map update creates a new map with structural sharing.
acceptance: translate_put_map_exact(builder, emitter, map, pairs, dest, fail_label) emits safepoint + jit_map_update call
jit_map_update returns new map or signals failure (key not found)
Failure branches to fail_label
Existing map is not modified (structural sharing)
Test: compiled #{a := 2} on #{a => 1} -> #{a => 2}
Test: compiled #{c := 3} on #{a => 1} -> branches to fail (key c not found)

requirements.title: Map pattern matching IR — get_map_elements and has_map_fields
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate get_map_elements into Cranelift IR that calls jit_map_get(process, map, key) for each requested key and stores the value in the destination register. IF any key is not found, THE SYSTEM SHALL branch to the fail label. THE SYSTEM SHALL translate has_map_fields into a series of jit_map_has_key(map, key) calls. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT emit GC safepoints for read-only map access.
acceptance: translate_get_map_elements(builder, map, keys_and_dests, fail_label) emits one jit_map_get per key
translate_has_map_fields(builder, map, keys, fail_label) emits one jit_map_has_key per key
jit_map_get(map, key) -> Option<u64> returns the value or None
Missing key branches to fail_label
No GC safepoints emitted (read-only operations)
Test: compiled case #{a => V} on #{a => 42} -> V = 42
Test: compiled case #{a => V} on #{b => 42} -> branches to fail

requirements.title: Remove map deoptimisation from opcode dispatch
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL update the main opcode dispatch to route map opcodes (put_map_assoc, put_map_exact, get_map_elements, has_map_fields) to ir_map.rs translations instead of emitting deopt exits. Prior to this brief, map opcodes triggered interpreter deoptimisation. THE SYSTEM SHALL leave map comprehension and map_size opcodes as deopt (out of scope for this brief).
acceptance: put_map_assoc, put_map_exact, get_map_elements, has_map_fields no longer trigger deopt
These opcodes route to ir_map.rs translations
map_size and map comprehension opcodes still deopt to interpreter
Test: function with map construction and pattern matching compiles and executes natively

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement map operations inline — all map ops go through runtime helpers
SHALL NOT implement map comprehensions in JIT — deoptimise to interpreter
SHALL NOT implement map_size in JIT — deoptimise to interpreter
SHALL NOT implement maps:merge or maps:fold in JIT — these are BIF calls handled via normal call dispatch
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
ir_map.rs is under 500 lines of production code
Map pattern matching does NOT emit GC safepoints
